references.title: Predicting Students Success in Blended Learning—Evaluating Different Interactions Inside Learning Management Systems

extracted.Features: [Count, Average, Median, Zeroed weeks, Average of the Difference, Commintment Factor, Cognitive Count, Teaching Count, Social Count, Other Count]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors do an aggregation of different actions into bigger constructs: Commitment, Cognitive, Teaching and Social.

Different datasets are constructed using different cmbination of variables. In total there are 13 configurations.

All datasets were oversampled using SMOTE.
extracted.Models: kNN, MLP, Naive Bayes, AdaBoost, Radndom Forest
extracted.Comments:
Authors create 13 datasets and run each dataset over 5 classifiers - 65 configurations in total. 

They create a model for each specific edition of the dataset and measure the Scores using AUC.
extracted.Limitations:
Small Sample Size

Single Course Context

No additional Data Sources

Only considers SMOTE for Resampling.

annotations.text: Courses such as algorithms and programming have very high rates of dropout. Authors create multiple early detection algorithms with different pre-processing techniques to identify which combinations generate the best predictions.

Authors find that, indeed, different features/algorithm combinations did not lead to statistically significant differences in predictive performance, ultimately finding that activity counts and some derived attributes are sufficient for the task. However, the authors did find that predictions in the second semester were much better → 0.7 AUC to 0.9 AUC. SMOTE did not improve performance of the models.
